label: No open exogenous-wildfire solar $/MW physical-severity benchmark exists
interpretation: HEADLINE: across all 63 catalogued sources and 100 benchmark rows, ZERO provide an isolated, properly-scoped exogenous (landscape-originated) wildfire solar $/MW physical-severity figure. Every normalized_usd_per_MW cell in the benchmark matrix is blank (never zero). The value grain below is RESOLVED from the canonical cell; the DR ordinates that would let a $/MW be placed on it are WITHHELD upstream (NO_RUNTIME_CURVE). There is nothing valid to recast.

label: ~1000 of ~80000 modules destroyed at a 20 MW exogenous-wildfire event (physical-unit anchor; ILLUSTRATIVE)
interpretation: ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY, NOT a benchmark. ~1,000 destroyed modules at a ~20 MW plant (~80,000 modules at ~250 W) implies a module-population burned fraction on the order of ~1% at THIS event; it is a unit-count anchor for the WS_MODULE_THERMAL failure unit, NOT a $/MW severity and NOT a DR ordinate. Firefighting-helicopter water also flooded inverters here, so even the physical damage blends fire + water pathways. Do NOT read a damage ratio from this.

label: Restoration savings $2.6M + $1.4M / avoided revenue (AVOIDED-COST framing)
interpretation: Vendor-reported AVOIDED-COST / restoration-savings figure (PORTFOLIO_BIAS), not a gross incurred-damage number. Cannot be normalized to $/MW severity or mapped to a DR. Context only for the replacement-fieldwork allocation rule.
